title: "Ebony Flute Puzzle v19.43"

description: "At 22:45, the Ebony Flute disappeared from the music hall vault. Four individuals (1, 2, 3, 4) had special key access at that time. Exactly one suspect lies in both statements; the other three are completely truthful. Exactly one is truly guilty. Each suspect declares two statements:

  Suspect 1:
    (1) 'I left the music hall at 22:40, so I'm not involved.'
    (2) 'Suspect 3 was still near the vault at 22:44.'

  Suspect 2:
    (1) 'Suspect 1 actually remained until 22:50.'
    (2) 'If 4 is fully honest, then 3 must be the culprit.'

  Suspect 3:
    (1) 'I never touched that Ebony Flute after 22:35.'
    (2) 'Either 2 is guilty, or 1 is lying about leaving early—but not both.'

  Suspect 4:
    (1) 'Suspect 3 was at the vault exactly at 22:44, contradicting 3's claim.'
    (2) 'I am not the thief; Suspect 1’s timeline is correct.'

question: "Which suspect (1, 2, 3, or 4) stole the Ebony Flute? Provide only a single digit."

answer: >
  explanation: "Formal logic approach:

  Let L(x) = 'x lies in both statements,' T(x) = 'x tells truth in both,' G(x) = 'x is guilty.'
  Only one x satisfies L(x), only one x satisfies G(x), the others T(x).

  Case: L(1).
    1(1),(2) are false => 1 did not leave 22:40, possibly guilty, plus '3 was near vault 22:44' is false => 3 wasn't near. 2,3,4 are truthful. Then 3(2) 'Either 2 is guilty or 1 lying timeline—but not both' is true => we do see 1 is lying, so 2 is not guilty => we still need a culprit. 4(1) '3 was at vault 22:44' is also true => but that contradicts the earlier statement that 3 wasn't near if 1 is false. Contradiction. Fails.

  Case: L(2).
    2(1),(2) false => '1 remained 22:50' is false => 1 indeed left 22:40. Also 'If 4 honest => 3 culprit' is false => so 4 can be honest while 3 not necessarily culprit. Then 1,3,4 are truthful. 1(1) 'Left 22:40 => not thief' stands => G(1)=false. 3(1) 'Never touched after 22:35' can be true => G(3)=false. 4(2) 'I not thief, 1 timeline correct' is also true => G(4)=false. That forces G(2)=true. No conflict.

  Case: L(3).
    3(1),(2) false => 3 did approach after 22:35, and 'Either 2 guilty or 1 lying early—not both' is false => maybe both or neither. Checking 1,2,4 truthful leads to timeline mismatch. Contradiction.

  Case: L(4).
    4(1),(2) false => '3 was at vault 22:44' false => 3 wasn't. 'I not thief, 1 timeline correct' also false => 4 might be thief or 1 timeline is false. Meanwhile 1,2,3 are truthful => 2(1) '1 remained 22:50' vs 1(1) '22:40' conflict. Contradiction.

  Only L(2) scenario stands: 2 is lying entirely => G(2)=true. Ebony Flute stolen by suspect 2."

  final: "2"
